brief.headline: When the LP problem moves past the store walls
brief.body: Three days of incidents and the pattern keeps showing up. Walgreens is not closing Chatham over shoplifting numbers. It is making a real estate call about what a store costs to keep open in a high-violence corridor. CHP did not bust the boosters who walked 600K of tools out of Home Depot. They busted the swap meet that bought the goods back. Everest did not ransomware a retailer. They hit the payment processor that thousands of merchants run on. And the Shreveport shooter was not a booster. He was an employee. The store walls are not where the perimeter sits anymore. AP teams who still organize around the four walls are reading from a map that does not match the territory.

callouts.label: Closure economics, South Side Chicago
callouts.text: Walgreens is shutting a decades-old Chatham pharmacy June 4. The Alderman wants modifications, not exit. Nobody is answering who pays for the fortified store.

callouts.label: Fence-first enforcement, Southern California
callouts.text: CHP recovered 600K in Home Depot tools by going after the swap meet, not the boosters. The resale channel was the choke point. Worth modeling.

callouts.label: Third-party payment risk, National
callouts.text: Everest ransomware listed Fiserv on its leak site May 3. Payment processing, merchant acquiring, core banking software for retailers. AP needs to call finance.

incidents.headline: Walgreens shutters Chatham store at 86th and Cottage Grove, blames theft and violence
incidents.summary: Walgreens told CBS Chicago the Chatham location ran significantly higher theft and violent incidents than peers, and it closes June 4. Alderman William Hall (6th Ward) is pushing for modifications instead of closure.

incidents.headline: $600K Home Depot fencing ring busted at SoCal swap meet
incidents.summary: California Highway Patrol's Southern Division ORC Task Force served warrants April 28 on two suspects and recovered $600,000 in stolen Home Depot tools. The pair bought from boosters and resold through a Los Angeles County swap meet that CHP also shut down.

incidents.headline: Everest ransomware crew claims Fiserv, the payment plumbing for US retailers
incidents.summary: Ransomware.live confirmed Fiserv on the Everest leak site May 3. Fiserv runs payment processing, merchant acquiring, and core banking software used by retailers and banks across the country, with no public timeline yet on data exfiltration or ransom demand.

incidents.headline: Shreveport grocery employee charged with attempted murder after shooting customer
incidents.summary: Malik Ahmad Omar, 21, an employee at Consumers Grocery on Hollywood Avenue, was arrested after surveillance confirmed he shot a customer inside the store during a dispute. The victim was found across the street with a life-threatening abdominal gunshot wound.

incidents.headline: Masked knife robbery at Newark Shore Stop in broad daylight
incidents.summary: A masked suspect with a knife held up the Shore Stop on Old Baltimore Pike in Newark, DE at 8:15 a.m. Saturday and walked out with cash from the register. No injuries. Delaware State Police Troop 2 working it.

incidents.headline: Bennington 99 Cents store held up at gunpoint Saturday morning
incidents.summary: Beshara's 99 Cents & Up Store on Park Street in Bennington, VT was held up at gunpoint at 10:43 a.m. Saturday. Suspect fled on foot toward Pleasant and Valentine. Bennington PD asking for video and tips.

incidents.headline: West Virginia Shell clerk's surveillance video goes national after shooter fires at counter and at the camera
incidents.summary: Rebecca Peterson, 42, of Oceana walked into a Lincoln County Shell around 4 a.m. Friday, told the clerk she wanted to rob the store, then fired at the back wall behind him and put another round into the surveillance camera. State troopers stopped her in Kanawha County.

incidents.headline: Traffic stop closes LaGrange Dollar Tree register grab inside 24 hours
incidents.summary: Ricky Pierce snatched cash from a Dollar Tree register on Commerce Avenue around 9:30 p.m. Friday after the clerk opened the drawer for him. LaGrange officers pulled Pierce over Saturday on a plate match from store cameras. He's charged with robbery.

incidents.headline: Chicago facial recognition tags Albany Park dollar store robber, same crew killed CPD officer four hours later
incidents.summary: Detectives ran Friday-night surveillance through facial recognition and identified Jeron Tate, 18, as the gunman's accomplice in an armed robbery of an Al
